prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response: Arrr, matey! The sky be blue ‘cause o’ a mighty trick o’ the sun’s light, it be. Ye see, when the great fiery orb shines upon our world, its light be made o’ many colors, but the blue bits be scattered all over the place by the sky’s tiny watery bits and dust—aye, like cannonballs bouncing off the sails! This scatterin’ spreads the blue far and wide, paintin’ the dome above our heads a grand ocean blue. ’Tis a right wonder of nature, like findin’ treasure beyond the horizon! So that’s why ye look up and see the sky wearin’ its finest blue coat, savvy?
